Evidence About Contact Disclaimer Swiss site

Evidence status

Brain cancers

Brain cancer research asks whether ketogenic metabolic therapy can stress tumour metabolism while supporting normal brain energy use. It remains adjunctive and investigational.

Evidence review Adjunctive and investigational Last reviewed: May 26, 2026

Scientific rationale

  • The rationale draws on altered tumour glucose metabolism, insulin and IGF signalling, mitochondrial stress, inflammation, and the brain's ability to use ketones.
  • Tumour biology is heterogeneous, so a single metabolic strategy is unlikely to apply equally across diagnoses.

Research snapshot

  • Glioblastoma studies suggest ketogenic therapy can be feasible in selected patients alongside standard oncology care, but trials remain small.
  • There is no established evidence that ketogenic diet alone improves survival, and it must not delay surgery, radiotherapy, chemotherapy, steroids, or trial care.

Clinical frame

  • Cancer patients may face cachexia, steroid-induced hyperglycaemia, nausea, dysphagia, seizures, and treatment-related fatigue, all of which change nutrition risk.
  • Any ketogenic strategy in brain cancer should be oncology-led and dietitian-supervised.

Open questions

  • Which tumour genotypes, imaging markers, or metabolic phenotypes identify patients worth studying?
  • How can trials separate ketosis, calorie intake, weight change, and standard treatment effects?

Selected evidence

  1. Efficacy and safety of ketogenic diet in glioblastoma: updated systematic review and meta-analysis Neurological Sciences, 2026
  2. Phase 1 safety and feasibility trial of a ketogenic diet plus standard of care for newly diagnosed glioblastoma Neuro-Oncology Advances, 2025
  3. Ketogenic metabolic therapy as an adjuvant to standard care in glioblastoma: systematic review of clinical trials Nutrients, 2025
  4. Ketogenic diet therapy for high-grade gliomas combined with standard treatment using an angiogenesis inhibitor Nutrients, 2026
  5. Systematic review and clinical insights: ketogenic diet in glioblastoma cancer neuroscience Journal of Personalized Medicine, 2024
  6. Clinical framework for ketogenic metabolic therapy in glioblastoma PubMed record, 2024
